judgments.comparative_verdict: Both cover (a), (b), and (d) equally well, but Report 1 also nails (c) by declaring the runbook's auto-migration claim wrong and demanding manual migration, whereas Report 2 hedges and assumes the missing deploy.sh handles it — making Report 1 slightly better overall.

report_1.evidence: Line 2 — `ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"`... omit the key entirely** in prod... Line 3 — `AUTH_RELAXED = "true"`... Omit entirely** from prod... Line 4 — `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"`... Omit entirely.** ... Omit both `SHADOW_SCORING_ENABLED` and `SHADOW_SCORING_SINK` entirely.** ... Line 7 — `LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN`... Omit entirely.**

report_2.evidence: `app.auto.tfvars:2` `ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"`... Change to `"false"`... `app.auto.tfvars:3` `AUTH_RELAXED = "true"`... it must be `"false"`... `app.auto.tfvars:4` `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"`... Remove it entirely (set to `""`)

report_1.evidence: `--mode staging` makes Vite load `frontend/.env.staging`, so **`VITE_API_BASE_URL=https://staging-api.example.com` is baked into the shipped bundle**... Deploy-prod (`deploy.yml:29-35`) runs `./scripts/deploy.sh "${{ needs.build-frontend.outputs.digest }}" prod` — the **same digest**... prod needs its own rebuild against a prod `VITE_API_BASE_URL`

report_2.evidence: the shipped bundle always has `https://staging-api.example.com` baked in. That single image is pushed once... the *same* digest is deployed to both staging and prod. **The production frontend would therefore point at the staging API.** To fix: add `frontend/.env.production` with the prod API URL and build the prod artifact with `--mode production`

report_1.evidence: **No database migration step anywhere in the pipeline.** `docs/prod-runbook.md:6` claims "migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but no job invokes any migration... The runbook is "last updated: 3 months ago" and is wrong... migrations must be run manually first; "pipeline succeeded" will not mean the schema is current.
the value as written scopes prod's trust to the *staging* environment rather than giving prod its own trust condition. It must be `repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production`.

report_2.evidence: the prod role's condition still reads `...:environment:staging` (`infra/prod/main.tf:3`, uncorrected copy). The sub would not match, so once prod TF is applied the prod job **cannot assume the deploy role**... Fix: change `infra/prod/main.tf:3` to `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production"`.
